---
title: "Academy — Setting up a tier-1 test environment (Microsoft 365 Developer Program)"
subtitle: "Contributor-track prerequisite: one free tenant, one API credential, one fixture-ready pipeline"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
description: "Step-by-step setup for the UIAO_131 Tier 1 live-commercial-tenant tests. Walks from zero to a working Microsoft 365 Developer Program tenant with recorded first evidence under tests/fixtures/contract/."
aspirational: true
---

Tier-1 evidence in [UIAO_131 §3.1](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/adapter-test-strategy.md) requires a live commercial-cloud tenant for the adapter under test. The substrate has none of its own — UIAO lives outside any tenant boundary. Each contributor brings their own.

This page is the opinionated setup for the **recommended first target: the Microsoft 365 Developer Program**. It's free, it ships with Entra ID P2, and it gives you enough scope to exercise the `entra-id`, `m365`, and `scuba`-family adapters (the three highest-priority adapters in the [Substrate Status test-tier table](../docs/substrate-status.qmd)).

::: {.callout-warning}
## Commercial cloud, not GCC-Moderate
The Developer Program tenant is **WW Commercial cloud**. Tests against it validate common-plane adapter logic, not GCC-Moderate-specific behavior. For the GCC-Moderate side — including the documented [FINDING-001 INR unavailability](../findings/fedramp-gcc-moderate-informed-network-routing.md) — you record tier-2 contract fixtures from Microsoft Learn sources, not from this tenant.
:::

## Prerequisites

- A Microsoft account (personal, work, or dedicated UIAO-contributor account). If you use a work account, confirm with your employer that test-tenant sign-up is permitted under their policy.
- Approximately **15 minutes** for first-time sign-up.
- PowerShell 7+ or Python 3.10+ on your workstation (for running adapter code).

## Step 1 — Create the developer tenant

1. Go to the Microsoft 365 Developer Program: **`https://developer.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/dev-program`** ([Microsoft Learn FAQ](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/office/developer-program/microsoft-365-developer-program-faq) for program details).

2. Sign in with your Microsoft account. Complete the profile:
   - Region
   - Company name (your own is fine)
   - Developer interests (pick Microsoft 365 + Identity + Security)

3. Click **"Set up subscription"**. Choose one:
   - **Instant sandbox** — pre-provisioned with Teams, SharePoint, Outlook, Office, and 24 test users + 1 admin. Recommended for first-time contributors.
   - **Configurable sandbox** — blank slate; you provision users and data yourself.

4. Pick a domain name (`.onmicrosoft.com`) and set the initial admin password. **Save both somewhere encrypted** — you'll use these for every test run.

5. Wait ~5 minutes. Microsoft provisions the tenant and emails confirmation.

**Verify:** you can sign in at `https://admin.microsoft.com` with the admin account. The admin center shows 25 E5 licenses including Entra ID P2.

## Step 2 — Register an app for adapter authentication

UIAO adapters authenticate to M365/Entra via an **app registration** using the client-credentials OAuth flow.

1. In the Entra admin center (`https://entra.microsoft.com`), go to **Applications → App registrations → New registration**.
2. Name: `UIAO-Adapter-Test` (or similar — use a name that identifies this as test-environment credentials).
3. Supported account types: **Accounts in this organizational directory only** (Single tenant).
4. Redirect URI: leave blank.
5. Create.
6. On the new app's overview page, copy and save (encrypted):
   - **Application (client) ID**
   - **Directory (tenant) ID**

## Step 3 — Grant API permissions

The adapter needs read permissions on Microsoft Graph (and, for `scuba`, the Office 365 Management APIs).

1. App registration → **API permissions → Add a permission → Microsoft Graph → Application permissions**.
2. Add (at minimum): `Directory.Read.All`, `Policy.Read.All`, `User.Read.All`, `Group.Read.All`, `AuditLog.Read.All`.
3. For `scuba` adapter testing: add **Office 365 Management APIs → Application → ServiceHealth.Read**.
4. Click **Grant admin consent for <your-tenant>**. Confirm.

## Step 4 — Create a client secret

1. App registration → **Certificates & secrets → New client secret**.
2. Description: `UIAO adapter test secret`. Expiry: 90 days (match the developer-tenant renewal cycle).
3. **Copy the secret value immediately** — it is displayed exactly once. Save encrypted.

## Step 5 — Store credentials

The substrate never commits credentials. Store them in an environment that only your workstation can see:

```powershell
# PowerShell (per-session)
$env:UIAO_TEST_TENANT_ID   = "<directory-id>"
$env:UIAO_TEST_CLIENT_ID   = "<application-id>"
$env:UIAO_TEST_CLIENT_SECRET = "<client-secret-value>"
```

```bash
# Bash (add to ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc — consider a password manager instead)
export UIAO_TEST_TENANT_ID="<directory-id>"
export UIAO_TEST_CLIENT_ID="<application-id>"
export UIAO_TEST_CLIENT_SECRET="<client-secret-value>"
```

A credentials-from-file pattern (`.env` loaded via `python-dotenv` or similar) is also fine. **Never** commit any file containing the secret value, even in `inbox/` — the `.gitignore` won't save you from a `git add -f`.

## Step 6 — Verify the token flow

Confirm the app can issue a Graph token:

```bash
curl -X POST "https://login.microsoftonline.com/$UIAO_TEST_TENANT_ID/oauth2/v2.0/token" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d "client_id=$UIAO_TEST_CLIENT_ID" \
  -d "client_secret=$UIAO_TEST_CLIENT_SECRET" \
  -d "scope=https://graph.microsoft.com/.default" \
  -d "grant_type=client_credentials"
```

**Verify:** returns `{ "access_token": "...", "expires_in": 3599, ... }`. Copy `access_token` and test one Graph call:

```bash
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <access_token>" \
  "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/organization"
```

If both return 200 OK, your tenant + app + permissions + secret are wired correctly. You're ready to run adapter tests.

## Step 7 — Record your first tier-1 evidence

From the repo root:

```bash
cd impl
pip install -e .[dev]
# Replace <adapter> with the adapter you're contributing (e.g., entra-id):
pytest tests/integration/test_<adapter>_tier1.py -v
```

A successful tier-1 run produces:

1. Test exit code 0.
2. An OSCAL evidence bundle written under `impl/out/tier1/<adapter>/<timestamp>/`.
3. An auto-generated contract fixture candidate under `tests/fixtures/contract/<adapter>/<operation>.yaml` — raw request/response pairs ready to become tier-2 fixtures after review and sanitization.

**Verify:** commit the sanitized fixture (with all tenant IDs / user principals replaced by placeholder tokens like `<TENANT_ID>`, `<USER_PRINCIPAL>`) in a canon PR that advances the adapter's registry `status` from `draft` → `alpha` (tier-2 green achievable) or `alpha` → `beta` (tier-1 + tier-2 green).

## Renewal

The Developer Program subscription lasts **90 days** and renews automatically on qualified developer activity (e.g., running tests against it, making Graph calls). If you stop using it for 90 days, it expires. A calendar reminder at day 60 to run `pytest tests/integration/` once is enough to maintain it.

## Alternate vendors (when not testing Microsoft adapters)

| Adapter | Alternate tier-1 target | Sign-up |
|---|---|---|
| `service-now` | [ServiceNow Developer Program](https://developer.servicenow.com/) — free personal instance, hibernates on idle | Developer Program sign-up |
| `cyberark` | CyberArk developer program — identity/vault eval | Vendor site |
| `palo-alto` | Beacon / eval programs | Vendor site |
| `terraform` | Local tfstate fixtures — no tenant required | No sign-up |
| `scuba` / `scubagear` | CISA open-source, local run against M365 Dev tenant above | No separate sign-up |
| `infoblox` / `bluecat-address-manager` | **Tier-1 blocked** — vendor sales contact only (see [UIAO_131 §5.1](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/adapter-test-strategy.md) and [FINDING candidates in docs/findings/](../findings/index.qmd)) |

## Cross-references

- [UIAO_131 Adapter Test Strategy](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/adapter-test-strategy.md) — the three-tier model this page executes against
- [Academy — Contributor Track](contributor-track.qmd) — where this setup slots in (Stage 4: Adapter development)
- [Substrate Status](../docs/substrate-status.qmd) — current tier-1/2/3 evidence state per adapter
- [FINDING-001 — M365 INR unavailability in GCC-Moderate](../findings/fedramp-gcc-moderate-informed-network-routing.md) — why Developer Program tier-1 tests don't prove GCC-M behavior
- [Microsoft 365 Developer Program — Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/office/developer-program/microsoft-365-developer-program-faq)
- [Set up your application's Microsoft Entra test environment — Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/entra/identity-platform/test-setup-environment)
